title: Secrets, Provider Data Terms, Privacy, Retention, and Sensitive Uses · Program Brief: Provider Data Terms
summary: An API key appears in source, prompts contain personal records, free and paid data terms are confused, logs persist indefinitely, and a high-impact use launches without review. Predict authority, model and API behavior, state, tool effects, budgets, privacy, safety, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the provider data terms increment in governed secret and agent-data lifecycle. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Record current paid, unpaid, logging, sharing, abuse-monitoring, region, age, and sensitive-use terms and re-review them before release.
Map purpose, legal or organizational authority, fields, sensitivity, users, provider transfer, storage, access, retention, deletion, and aggregation risk.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

Build deterministic Python evidence to enforce secret custody, data minimization, provider terms, retention, deletion, regional and sensitive-use review, and redacted evidence; telemetry and memory must remain tenant-isolated, minimized, redacted, and attributable; then change one eval case, rubric, model version, token price, rate limit, or latency distribution and rerun release gates. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. A defensible solution applies Provider Data Terms, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to governed secret and agent-data lifecycle.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-worked-agentpy-secrets-privacy-governance-4
# Competency: agentpy-worked-agentpy-secrets-privacy-governance-4.
# Case 1655: a controlled support-operations rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: telemetry and memory must remain tenant-isolated, minimized, redacted, and attributable.
# Changed case: change one eval case, rubric, model version, token price, rate limit, or latency distribution and rerun release gates.
def worked_agentpy_secrets_privacy_governance_4(data_fields: int = 28, approved_fields: int = 12, rejected_fields: int = 16, redacted_logs: int = 28, leaked_secrets: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "authorityworked4",
        "interaction1655",
        "tool4dekrow",
        "stateworked41655",
        "evaluation1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if data_fields < 1 or approved_fields < 0 or rejected_fields < 0:
        return False, data_fields, "privacy-field-count"
    if approved_fields + rejected_fields != data_fields:
        return False, approved_fields, "data-minimization"
    if redacted_logs != data_fields:
        return False, redacted_logs, "log-redaction"
    if leaked_secrets > 0:
        return False, leaked_secrets, "secret-leak"
    assert approved_fields + rejected_fields == data_fields and leaked_secrets == 0
    return True, approved_fields, "governed-agent-data"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Build Provider Data Terms evidence · Theory Provider Data Terms
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-provider-data-terms-kozni6" that uses case 4752 to enforce secret custody, data minimization, provider terms, retention, deletion, regional and sensitive-use review, and redacted evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-provider-data-terms-kozni6
# Competency: agentpy-provider-data-terms.
# Case 4752: a privacy-minimized nonprofit workflow.
# Operating constraint: model output and tool arguments remain untrusted until validated.
# Changed case: replace trusted context with untrusted indirect instructions and preserve data-versus-instruction boundaries.
def evidence_agentpy_provider_data_terms_kozni6(data_fields: int = 28, approved_fields: int = 12, rejected_fields: int = 16, redacted_logs: int = 28, leaked_secrets: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_kozni6 = "kozni6-6inzok-4752"
    assert evidence_variant_kozni6
    evidence_axes_kozni6 = (
        "authoritykozni6",
        "interaction4752",
        "tool6inzok",
        "statekozni64752",
        "evaluation4752kozni6",
        "recovery6inzok4752",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_kozni6)) == 6
    if data_fields < 1 or approved_fields < 0 or rejected_fields < 0:
        return False, data_fields, "privacy-field-count"
    if approved_fields + rejected_fields != data_fields:
        return False, approved_fields, "data-minimization"
    if redacted_logs != data_fields:
        return False, redacted_logs, "log-redaction"
    if leaked_secrets > 0:
        return False, leaked_secrets, "secret-leak"
    assert approved_fields + rejected_fields == data_fields and leaked_secrets == 0
    return True, approved_fields, "governed-agent-data"
